text: Reasoning is a key capacity of the human mind and essential to many cognitive tasks such as problem solving, decision making and thinking critically. Recently, large language models (LLMs) have made impressive strides in the field of natural language processing (NLP) and there is growing consensus that larger models can often reason when they are capable of carrying out the task in the first place. However, the extent to which an LLM is able to reason is still a matter of debate. This paper presents a full review of the state of the art in respect of LLMs and reasoning, including ways of improving and eliciting reasoning, as well as the methods and benchmarks for assessing the reasoning capacity of LLMs. We summarize key findings and implications of existing work on this topic, and provide a number of recommendations for future work on this topic with the goal of providing a comprehensive and timely review that will serve to further discussion and activity in this field.
headline: Fully Human Written
